raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/2023, I signed up for XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX ( a service NOT a good ). While on the phone with XXXX, the sales XXXX XXXXXXXX, stated on the recorded XXXX call and via email that I would be subject to a money-back guarantee if I did not earn XXXX in income while in the program if I completed all tasks required. Per my XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX contract as stated in section 1 : SERVICES, which is attached and highlighted ( XXXX XXXXXXXX GUARANTEE : *Company guarantees that Client will generate at least {$15000.00} in total gross revenue while inside this Program ). I have done everything my contract has required and beyond, and I have not reached XXXX in revenue to date and would like/ask that the money-back guarantee be enforced. Again, this is a service and not a good one. All information about the money-back guarantee is located on pages XXXX ; I have highlighted that information. I have attached all other proofs in the previous claim. Please let me know if you need that info again, and I will gladly resend it. The rep on the phone just advised me to send the contract and highlight the money-back guarantee portion. According to the FTC, this fraud is considered fraudulent/deceptive business practices, and the charge should be treated as fraud. I have reported this to the FTC and XXXX
